findings.reachability: Authenticated administrators only (manage_options capability required via ajax_run_tool at line 22)
findings.justification: Both $secret_key and $response are sanitized with sanitize_text_field() (which does not encode & or = characters) then directly concatenated into the Google reCAPTCHA siteverify URL, allowing an admin to inject additional query parameters and manipulate the outbound verification request.

findings.reachability: Authenticated administrators only (manage_options check at line 247); requires attacker-controlled data in the reason column of the accesslocks table
findings.justification: The 'reason' column value from the DB is echoed into the JSON output array without any HTML escaping at line 352 ($row[] = $aRow->$col for $col === 'reason'); in the current codebase reason is populated only with hardcoded strings, but any future code path that stores arbitrary input there would create stored XSS in the admin DataTable.

findings.reachability: Authenticated administrators (manage_options) viewing the locks log DataTable
findings.justification: The DB integer field $aRow->accesslock_ID is embedded into HTML attribute values (data-lock-id="...") without esc_attr(); while the column is numeric in practice, any corruption or unexpected value could break the attribute context.
